title: 360Ghar Platform Policies and Legal Documentation
content:
360Ghar Platform Policies and Legal Documentation

Last Updated: September 2025

title: Terms of Service
content:
Terms of Service

1. Acceptance of Terms

By downloading, accessing, or using the 360Ghar mobile app, website, or any related services (“Services”), you agree to these Terms of Service (“Terms”). These Terms constitute a binding legal agreement between you and 360Ghar (operated by YORD LLC, USA, doing business as 360Ghar in India). If you do not agree with any part of these Terms, you must not use the Services. You affirm that you are at least 18 years old and legally competent to contract under Indian law (Indian Contract Act, 1872) ￼. If you are using the Services on behalf of an entity, you represent that you have authority to bind that entity to these Terms.

2. Services Overview

360Ghar is a next-generation real estate platform for Indian properties. We provide an online venue for property owners (“Owners”) and seekers (“Users”) to interact. Key features include 360-degree virtual property tours, a swipe interface to like or pass properties, map-based property discovery, and scheduling of property visits via the app. Our team directly onboards properties by photographing them, verifying details, and collecting accurate location data to ensure authentic listings. Important: 360Ghar is a facilitator; all final real estate transactions (sale, rent, etc.) are agreements directly between the Buyer and Seller or Licensor and Licensee, as applicable ￼. 360Ghar is not a party to any sale/rental agreement between users, and does not own the properties listed.

3. Account Registration and Security

To use certain features, you must create an account with accurate personal information. Required information at sign-up includes your full name, email, mobile number, date of birth, gender, and responses to onboarding questions; property Owners may need to provide property documents, ownership proof, and a government-issued ID for verification ￼. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account login credentials and all activities under your account ￼. You agree to immediately notify 360Ghar of any unauthorized use of your account or security breach ￼. We reserve the right to refuse registration or terminate accounts at our discretion if these Terms are violated.

4. User Obligations and Acceptable Use

By using our Services, you agree to the following obligations:
	•	Provide True Information: You represent and warrant that all information you provide (including property details if you are an Owner, and any personal or contact info) is truthful, accurate, and up-to-date ￼. You will promptly update any information that becomes outdated or incorrect. You must possess all necessary rights and licenses to any content (e.g. property photos) you upload, and by posting such content you grant 360Ghar a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use and display it on our platform ￼.
	•	One Account; Personal Use: You may create only one account for your personal use (or one account per entity you officially represent). You will not impersonate any person or entity or misrepresent your affiliation in creating an account ￼. You shall not share your login credentials with others or allow others to use your account ￼.
	•	Compliance with Laws: You agree to use the Services only for lawful purposes and in compliance with all applicable laws and regulations of India. This includes real estate regulations such as the Real Estate (Regulation and Development) Act, 2016 (RERA) for property transactions, and any local laws. If you are an Owner or broker, you are responsible for ensuring compliance with RERA (e.g., registering the project if required) before listing. Users must verify all property details, including RERA registration numbers and ownership documents, before finalizing any transaction ￼. 360Ghar strives to list only legally compliant properties, but does not guarantee that every listing meets all regulatory requirements; Users rely on listings at their own risk and should perform due diligence ￼.
	•	Prohibited Activities: You shall NOT engage in any of the following when using 360Ghar:
	•	Circumvention of Platform: You must not attempt to negotiate or finalize property deals outside of the 360Ghar platform with a party introduced through 360Ghar for the purpose of avoiding or reducing the service fees/brokerage due to 360Ghar. If you are introduced to a buyer/seller or landlord/tenant via 360Ghar, you agree not to complete a transaction with that party except through 360Ghar’s facilitation and with payment of the applicable service charge to 360Ghar. Any attempt to circumvent 360Ghar in this manner is a material breach of these Terms. If a user directly or indirectly circumvents 360Ghar and enters into a transaction with another party introduced by 360Ghar, 360Ghar shall be entitled to its full brokerage fee from that user, notwithstanding any other arrangements made by the user to avoid such payment ￼. 360Ghar reserves the right to recover such fees and take legal action in such cases.
	•	Unauthorized Use of Content: You will not copy, scrape, crawl, reproduce, republish, or exploit any data or content on 360Ghar for commercial purposes without our prior written consent ￼ ￼. The platform’s design, databases, images, and text are protected intellectual property. You receive a limited, revocable license to use the Services for your personal real estate needs. Any attempt to reverse engineer, decompile, or tamper with the app/website, or to bypass any technical protections is strictly prohibited ￼ ￼.
	•	Interference and Security: You shall not use any robot, spider, site search/retrieval application, or other automated means to extract or index any portion of the Service (scraping) without 360Ghar’s express permission ￼. You must not attempt to gain unauthorized access to the Services or its related systems or networks, or probe or test the vulnerability of our security measures ￼. Introducing any viruses, worms, malware, or any other destructive code is forbidden ￼.
	•	Misuse and Harassment: You will not use the Services to harass, threaten, or harm 360Ghar staff, our field agents, other users, or any third parties. This includes refraining from abusive language, hate speech, or aggressive behavior during property visits or communication. Any form of violence, abuse, or misbehavior towards our team is strictly prohibited. If you engage in such conduct, 360Ghar may immediately terminate your account and services, and you agree to compensate 360Ghar for any losses or damages resulting from your misconduct. You may also be subject to legal consequences under applicable law.
	•	Content Standards: You are solely responsible for any content you post (such as property reviews, comments, or other user-generated content). Such content must adhere to our Content Guidelines (see Content Guidelines below) and not violate any law or rights of others. Prohibited content includes anything illegal, defamatory, obscene, invasive of privacy, misleading, or harmful (detailed in the Content Guidelines section) ￼ ￼. 360Ghar reserves the right to remove or moderate any user content that violates these Terms or our policies, or for any reason in our sole discretion, without notice ￼.
	•	Platform Cooperation: You agree to comply with any instructions or rules provided on the platform. You will not disrupt the normal flow of the application (e.g., by repeatedly posting duplicate information, or by engaging in spamming, or by any actions that impose an unreasonable load on our infrastructure) ￼. If you are an Owner or property manager using a special dashboard or tools provided by 360Ghar, you will use those only for managing your own listings and not for any unauthorized purpose. 360Ghar may monitor usage and content to ensure compliance and quality.

5. Services Fee, Payment, and Non-Circumvention

Brokerage/Service Charges: 360Ghar provides end-to-end assistance in real estate transactions and charges a service fee (brokerage) upon successful deals. For property sales, 360Ghar charges a commission of 1% of the sale price from the Seller and 1% from the Buyer (total 2%). For property rentals (lease/license), 360Ghar charges 50% of one month’s rent from the Landlord (Licensor) and 50% of one month’s rent from the Tenant (Licensee). These fees are due and payable upon the parties entering a binding sale agreement or lease agreement, respectively. By using our Services and consummating a transaction introduced via 360Ghar, you agree to pay the applicable fees. Owners may be required to pay a small advance fee when listing a property to initiate on-site onboarding (photography, verification etc.); any such advance will either be adjusted against the final brokerage due at deal closure or refunded/waived as per promotional offers or at 360Ghar’s discretion.

No Bypassing 360Ghar: As noted in Section 4, users (both property owners and seekers) must not bypass 360Ghar to avoid paying service charges. If you complete a sale or rental with a party you discovered through 360Ghar without routing the deal through us or without paying our fees, you will be in violation of these Terms. In such an event, 360Ghar reserves the right to calculate the fee that would have been due and demand payment from you. You agree that 360Ghar’s determination of the fee and breach will be final and binding, and you will be liable to pay such fee along with any legal costs incurred in recovery ￼. Furthermore, any transaction completed in this manner, without settling the service charge, may be deemed invalid by 360Ghar – we reserve the right to refuse to recognize or support such a transaction, and we may ban the offending parties from the platform.

No Refund Policy for Completed Deals: Service charges (brokerage) are considered earned by 360Ghar upon the execution of sale/rental agreements between the parties. These charges are non-refundable once a deal is concluded. If a deal falls through due to user fault or mutual disagreement after 360Ghar’s services were rendered, 360Ghar may, at its sole discretion, decide on any refund or fee adjustment on a case-by-case basis, but is not obligated to do so. For any upfront listing fees or advances paid by Owners, refundability (if the property doesn’t sell/rent) will be as per any listing agreement or specific terms communicated during listing; otherwise, such fees may be treated as non-refundable service charges for the onboarding effort.

Short Stay and Future Services: If 360Ghar introduces short-stay rentals or other value-added services (e.g. interior design, loan assistance, legal documentation services) where payments are handled online via our platform, separate terms (including payment terms, cancellation/refund policies) will be provided within the app for those services. By using those services, you agree to the additional terms. In absence of separate terms, these Terms apply. All online payments will be processed via approved payment gateways and you must provide accurate billing information. 360Ghar is not responsible for payment gateway downtime or errors.

6. Property Listings and Platform Role

Listing Authenticity: 360Ghar’s team strives to verify and photograph each property listing. However, except where explicitly stated, information on listings is provided by Owners or gathered from them. 360Ghar does not guarantee that all property details (including availability, pricing, measurements, legal title, or approvals) are accurate or error-free ￼ ￼. Users should independently verify crucial details (such as ownership documents, property area, or any required governmental approvals) before making decisions. By using the platform, you acknowledge that any reliance on listings is at your own risk ￼. Owners are expected to provide only accurate information and have all rights to list the property; any false or misleading listing may be removed and the Owner may be liable for any losses or legal issues arising from such misinformation ￼.

Platform License for Listings: By creating a property listing or submitting content to 360Ghar, the Owner grants 360Ghar a worldwide, royalty-free, sublicensable license to use, reproduce, modify (e.g. for formatting), and display the content (e.g., photos, descriptions) on our platform and in marketing materials, for the purpose of showcasing the property and providing services to users ￼. This license lasts until the content is removed from the platform. Owners represent that the content is owned by them or they have permission to grant this license, and that the content does not infringe any third-party rights ￼.

No Endorsement or Agency: 360Ghar is not an agent or fiduciary of any user, except that it facilitates communications and assists in paperwork between parties. While 360Ghar has field agents (“360Ghar Team”) who show properties and assist in negotiations, these agents act to facilitate the transaction and do not create any agency relationship with either party that would make 360Ghar a legal representative of the buyer or seller. 360Ghar does not guarantee that buyers or tenants will perform their obligations or that sellers or landlords will honor their commitments. We do not endorse any user or property; we only provide a platform and preliminary verification. All decisions about engaging in a transaction are made solely by the users. 360Ghar will not be liable for any default, fraud, misrepresentation, or failure to complete a transaction by any party introduced through our platform ￼ ￼.

Scheduling Visits: Users can request property site visits through the app. 360Ghar will coordinate with the Owner and confirm a visit time. Owners agree to provide reasonable access to their property for scheduled visits. Users must behave professionally and safely during site visits. Any damage caused by a visitor during a site visit is the visitor’s responsibility. 360Ghar team may accompany the visitor, but the ultimate arrangement of a site visit (e.g., property access, keys) is the Owner’s responsibility ￼. 360Ghar is not liable for any incident, injury, or loss that occurs during a site visit – visitors enter properties at their own risk and should exercise due caution.

7. Content and Community Guidelines

All users must adhere to the Content Guidelines (see below) when posting any content (such as reviews, comments, or descriptions). Prohibited content or activity includes, but is not limited to, the following non-exhaustive list, which incorporates the requirements of India’s IT Act and rules ￼ ￼:
	•	Content that belongs to someone else and is posted without rights or permission (e.g., using someone else’s photos or description without consent) ￼.
	•	Content that is defamatory (damaging someone’s reputation with false information), obscene, pornographic, or paedophilic in nature ￼. This includes any indecent or sexually explicit material.
	•	Content that infringes any intellectual property rights including copyright, trademark, patent, or trade secrets of a third party ￼. (For example, do not upload floor plans or images from another website unless you have rights.)
	•	Material that is invasive of another’s privacy, including personal information of others without consent, or breaches someone’s bodily privacy ￼. This also covers harassing or insulting content targeted at someone’s gender, or any personal attribute.
	•	Hate speech or harassment: Content that is insulting or incites hostility toward any individual or group based on race, religion, caste, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, etc., is strictly forbidden ￼. Likewise, any form of bullying, stalking, or trolling other users or our staff is not allowed.
	•	False or misleading information: You must not knowingly post false information or impersonate another person. Do not deceive others about the origin of your posts, or spread information that is patently false or misleading in nature and could be perceived as factual ￼ ￼. For instance, posting fake property listings, false reviews, or false claims (like “sold by owner” when it’s not) is prohibited.
	•	Content that promotes illegal activities or is otherwise unlawful. This includes any information that could constitute or encourage a criminal offense, violate any law, or provide instructions for illegal activities. Any content relating to money laundering, gambling, or other unlawful transactions is disallowed ￼.
	•	Content that threatens the unity, integrity, defense, security, or sovereignty of India, friendly relations with foreign states, or public order, or is capable of causing incitement to any cognizable offense or prevents investigation of any offense, or is insulting to another nation ￼. In short, any content that could fall foul of national security or public order laws is banned.
	•	Content that could be considered spam or advertising: Unsolicited promotions, repetitive postings, or content that is unrelated to real estate in the context of our platform are not allowed. You may not advertise competing services or post referral codes, links, or promotional content for anything outside 360Ghar without permission.
	•	Malicious software or code: You must not upload or share any files that contain viruses, trojans, worms, spyware, malware, or other harmful code that could damage or interrupt the Services or other users’ devices ￼. Any attempt to phish or collect personal data from others through deceptive means is forbidden.
	•	Any content that otherwise violates any law in force ￼ or these Terms.

360Ghar reserves the right (but not the obligation) to pre-screen, refuse, or remove any user content that violates these guidelines or for any other reason at our sole discretion. We may also disable or suspend the accounts of repeat violators or those who post extremely egregious content. Users shall remain solely responsible for the content they post and may be held legally accountable for unlawful content (e.g., under defamation, copyright, or IT Act provisions). If you come across any content that violates our guidelines or appears unlawful, you can report it as described in the Takedown Policy section.

8. Privacy and Communications

Your privacy is important to us. Please refer to our Privacy Policy (below) for details on how we collect, use, store, and disclose your personal information. By using the Services, you consent to our collection and use of personal data as outlined in the Privacy Policy ￼.

Communication Consent: By registering with 360Ghar, you consent to receive communications from us or our partners/agents via phone calls, SMS, email, WhatsApp, or in-app notifications related to the Services. You agree that we may send you informational as well as promotional messages about property listings or related services, even if your number is on Do-Not-Disturb (DND) registries ￼. We will ensure such communications comply with applicable telecom regulations. If you prefer not to receive promotional communications, you may opt-out by following the unsubscribe instructions in those messages or by adjusting your account settings. However, you may still receive transactional messages (e.g., visit confirmations, account alerts) until your account is deleted.

360Ghar (or its agents) may also contact you to: understand your requirements, offer assistance in your property search or sale, provide updates on deals you are involved in, or gather feedback. Such calls may be recorded for quality and training purposes and to assist in fulfilling your requirements. By accepting these Terms, you give us express consent to make such calls or messages and to record them for the purposes stated ￼. If you do not wish to be contacted for promotions, please use the opt-out methods provided; note that opting out of promotional contact may limit your awareness of new features or deals.

9. Limitation of Liability

Service “As Is”: The Services are provided on an “as is” and “as available” basis, without warranty of any kind. 360Ghar and its affiliates expressly disclaim all warranties, whether express or implied, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement ￼ ￼. We do not guarantee that the Services will meet your requirements or be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free ￼. While we aim for accuracy, we do not warrant the accuracy or reliability of any information obtained through the Service (such as property details, pricing, availability, or user reviews) ￼. Use of the platform is at your own risk.

No Liability for Transactions: 360Ghar is not involved in the final transaction between users, and has no control over the quality, safety, legality, or integrity of the properties or the parties. We cannot and do not guarantee that a buyer or tenant will complete a transaction or that a seller or landlord will convey title or lease as agreed ￼. Any dispute between users is solely between those parties, though we may try to facilitate resolution. 360Ghar takes no responsibility and assumes no liability for any disputes, claims, losses, or damages arising out of transactions between users. In the event of a dispute between you and another user, you release 360Ghar (and our officers, employees, and agents) from all claims, demands, and damages arising out of or in any way connected to such dispute ￼. We are not obligated to mediate or resolve disputes, but if we choose to provide input, any suggestion or decision by 360Ghar is offered only to assist and shall not make 360Ghar liable for the outcome.

Consequential Damages Waiver: Under no circumstances will 360Ghar or its directors, employees, or agents be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or exemplary damages arising from or relating to your use of or inability to use the Services (including but not limited to loss of profits, data loss, or business interruption), even if we have been advised of the possibility of such damages ￼ ￼. This limitation applies to any damages stemming from any transactions or interactions with other users or third-party service providers accessed through 360Ghar.

Liability Cap: To the fullest extent permitted by law, the maximum aggregate liability of 360Ghar (including our parent company and affiliates) for all claims arising from your use of the Services or these Terms shall not exceed the amount (if any) you have paid to 360Ghar as service fees in the 2 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim ￼. If you have paid no fees, 360Ghar’s liability shall be zero, to the extent allowed by law. This limitation is cumulative and not per-incident.

Release of “Deficiency of Service” Claims: You agree that, given the nature of the Services (facilitative platform and not guaranteed outcomes), you shall not bring any claim against 360Ghar for deficiency of service under the Consumer Protection Act or any other law for any issue arising out of the Services ￼. In the event of any technical failure, downtime, data loss, or other service shortcomings, 360Ghar will endeavor to assist but will not be liable for any compensation beyond, at most, refunding any amount you paid for an undelivered paid feature (if applicable) ￼.

Third-Party Services: The Services may integrate or connect with third-party services (for example, map services, payment gateways, legal document providers, or social media for login). 360Ghar is not responsible for the availability or accuracy of third-party services, nor the content, products, or services on or available from such third parties. Your use of third-party services (e.g., clicking a link to a legal service provider or using a payment processor) is at your own risk and may be subject to that third party’s terms. 360Ghar disclaims liability for any acts or omissions of third-party service providers, and you agree that 360Ghar is not liable for any loss or claim you may have against any third party.

No Warranty of Data Security: While 360Ghar takes industry-standard measures to protect data, we do not warrant that the platform will be free from data loss, hacks, viruses, or security breaches. Any transmission of data via our app or website is at your own risk. See the Privacy Policy for details on our security practices.

10. Indemnification

You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless 360Ghar, its parent company (YORD LLC) and affiliates, and each of their respective officers, directors, employees, and agents, from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) that arise out of or relate to: (a) your use or misuse of the Services; (b) your breach of any provision of these Terms or any policy incorporated herein; (c) your violation of any law or regulation or of any rights of any third party (including intellectual property or privacy rights) through the platform; or (d) any content you provide or actions you take that cause harm to someone or to 360Ghar. This obligation will survive termination of your account or these Terms.

In the event a third-party lawsuit or regulatory action is brought against 360Ghar due to your conduct, 360Ghar will have full authority to defend or settle the matter, and you will cooperate fully as reasonably required ￼ ￼. You may not settle any claim against 360Ghar without our prior written consent. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, you unilaterally agree to indemnify and hold 360Ghar (and its officers, directors, employees, and agents) harmless from any claims or demands, including legal fees, arising out of your use of 360Ghar or your breach of these Terms ￼. This includes, for example, any claim by another user that you failed to pay the required brokerage or that you posted defamatory content, etc., or any governmental penalty due to illegal content you posted.

11. Termination of Service

These Terms are effective unless and until terminated by either you or 360Ghar. You may stop using the Services at any time by deleting your account. 360Ghar may, in its sole discretion, suspend or terminate your account and access to Services at any time, with or without notice, for any valid reason including but not limited to: violation of these Terms or policies, extended periods of inactivity, your request to delete account, or technical or security issues. We may also remove or disable any content you posted, and/or ban you from creating new accounts, at our sole discretion ￼. In case of termination due to your breach, you remain liable for any obligations (such as payment of outstanding fees or damages) accrued before termination.

If you are a paid customer for any premium features, and we terminate without cause, we may provide a pro-rated refund for any remaining unused period of a prepaid service (if applicable). However, no refund is due if termination is for your breach of Terms or misuse of the platform.

Upon termination, Sections regarding Limitation of Liability, Indemnity, Governing Law, Dispute Resolution, and any other clauses which by their nature should survive will continue to be in effect.

12. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution

These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, without regard to its conflict of law principles. The courts of Gurgaon, Haryana, India shall have exclusive jurisdiction over all disputes arising from or relating to these Terms or the Services ￼. You agree that any such dispute shall be filed only in the appropriate courts located in Gurgaon (Gurugram) District, and you hereby consent and submit to the personal jurisdiction of those courts.

Waiver of Certain Actions: To the extent permitted under applicable law, you agree not to initiate or participate in any class action or joint litigation concerning any dispute with 360Ghar. All claims will be pursued on an individual basis. You also agree that you will not sue 360Ghar or its associates in any jurisdiction other than Gurgaon, and any legal action brought elsewhere in violation of this clause shall be null and void or transferred to Gurgaon courts upon our request. Users agree that they will not attempt to hold 360Ghar liable or sue for matters that the users have agreed are under 360Ghar’s discretion or for which liability is disclaimed by these Terms. In any event, the parties shall first attempt to resolve disputes amicably through negotiations or mediation before resorting to litigation.

(For clarity: This clause does not restrict a user’s rights under law to seek redress, but it defines the forum and means by which claims should be raised. Users also explicitly agree that 360Ghar’s decisions in operational matters (such as content removal, fee assessment for circumvention, etc.) are final, conclusive, and binding in the context of using the platform.)

13. Miscellaneous Provisions
	•	Changes to Terms: 360Ghar may amend or update these Terms from time to time. We will notify users of material changes via app/website notification or email. Updated terms will be effective upon posting unless stated otherwise. Continued use of the Services after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised Terms ￼. If you do not agree to the changes, you should discontinue use of the Services.
	•	Entire Agreement: These Terms, along with the Privacy Policy and any other policies or guidelines posted on the platform, constitute the entire agreement between you and 360Ghar regarding the use of Services, superseding any prior agreements. Any waiver of any provision of the Terms will be effective only if in writing and signed by 360Ghar.
	•	Severability: If any provision of these Terms is held to be invalid or unenforceable, such provision shall be struck out or modified to the minimum extent necessary, and the remaining provisions shall remain in full force and effect.
	•	No Waiver: 360Ghar’s failure to enforce any right or provision of these Terms shall not constitute a waiver of such right or provision unless acknowledged and agreed by us in writing.
	•	Assignment: You may not assign or transfer your rights or obligations under these Terms to any third party without our prior written consent. 360Ghar may freely assign these Terms (for instance, in the event of a merger, acquisition, or internal reorganization) without notice or consent.
	•	Relationship: Nothing in these Terms shall be deemed to constitute a partnership, joint venture, or employment relationship between you and 360Ghar. You and 360Ghar are independent contracting parties. You have no authority to bind 360Ghar or represent 360Ghar as an agent in any way.
	•	Notices: Any notices or communications to users may be made via email, postal mail, notification on our app/website, or any other contact method you provide. Official notices to us (e.g., legal notices) should be sent to the contact provided in our Grievance Redressal section, unless a specific legal notice address is given. Notices will be considered received: (a) if by email, when the email is sent (provided no bounce or error is received); (b) if by courier, when delivered; (c) if by registered post, on delivery confirmation; or (d) if by platform notification, on your next login or 24 hours after posting, whichever is sooner.

title: Privacy Policy
content:
Privacy Policy

Effective Date: September 2025

1. Introduction

360Ghar (“Company” or “we” or “us” or “our”) values the trust of our users and is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, share, and safeguard personal information in connection with our Services (as defined in the Terms of Service). It also outlines your rights and choices regarding your personal data. This Policy is in compliance with applicable Indian laws, including the Information Technology Act, 2000 and rules thereunder, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023 (DPDP Act), and other relevant regulations.

By using 360Ghar’s Services, you consent to the collection and use of your information as explained in this Policy ￼. If you do not agree, please refrain from using the Services. This Policy is incorporated into and subject to our Terms of Service.

2. Information We Collect

We collect “Personal Data” (information that can identify you) and “Non-Personal Data” (information that cannot directly identify you). The following categories detail what we collect:

A. Information You Provide Directly
	•	Account Registration Data: When you create an account or fill forms on 360Ghar, we ask for basic contact and profile details. This includes your full name, email address, phone number, date of birth, gender, and password. We may also request your postal address or city to customize search results. If you register via social media or Google, we receive the basic profile info you authorize (such as name and email). For example, MagicBricks (an online real estate platform) similarly collects name, contact number, age, address, etc., during registration ￼.
	•	Identity and Verification Data (Property Owners): If you list a property with us, we require additional information to verify authenticity. This may include property ownership documents, title deed or registry copies, government-issued photo ID (e.g., PAN, Aadhaar, Passport), recent utility bills for address proof, and any relevant RERA registration number for new developments. We collect these to ensure the listing is legitimate and to build trust with buyers.
	•	Property Listing Details: Owners provide extensive information about their property: property type, address and exact location coordinates, photographs, videos or 360° tour imagery (captured by us or provided by you), floor plans, amenities, price, rental terms, maintenance fees, description, etc. Some information (like exact address or interior photos) might be considered personal to the owner; by providing it, you consent to our using and publishing it as needed for prospective buyers/tenants.
	•	Profile and Preferences: We may collect additional details when you fill out your profile or answer onboarding questions – for example, your property preferences (budget range, location preference, property type needed), family size, occupation, etc. This helps us personalize your experience (e.g., showing nearby schools if you have children). Providing these is optional.
	•	Communications with Us: If you contact us (via support email, phone, chat, or ticket), we collect the information and any attachments you provide in those communications. For instance, if you email support to raise an issue, we will collect the content of your message and your contact details to respond.
	•	Feedback and Reviews: If you participate in surveys, provide feedback, or review a property or our services, we collect your input which may include personal opinions and experiences.
	•	Transactions and Payments: Currently, for real estate brokerage transactions, payments (commission, etc.) are handled offline. If in the future we facilitate online payments (e.g., for short stay bookings or any upfront fees), we or our payment gateway may collect your payment details such as billing address or partial credit card information (note: full card info is typically collected by the payment gateway, not stored on our servers). We will also record transaction history such as amounts, dates, and what service it was for.

B. Information We Collect Automatically
When you use 360Ghar’s app or website, we automatically collect certain technical information:
	•	Device and Log Information: We log details about your usage of the Services, including your IP address, device type (e.g., iPhone, Android phone, or PC), device identifiers or advertising ID, operating system version, app version, browser type (if web), pages or screens viewed, actions taken (like filters applied or properties liked), the referring webpage, and access times ￼. For example, we might record that you searched for “3 BHK in Gurgaon” and clicked on certain listings at a specific time.
	•	Location Data: With your permission, we collect precise geolocation data of your device. This can help show your location relative to properties on the map or suggest nearby listings. If you grant background location permission, we may gather location updates to provide location-based services (you can disable this in your device settings). For property Owners, we collect the property’s location coordinates for mapping and verifying proximity to landmarks.
	•	Cookies and Similar Tech: We use cookies and similar tracking technologies on our website to enhance user experience. Cookies are small text files stored on your browser. They help us recognize repeat visitors, store preferences (like saved searches), and track user trends. For instance, a cookie might save your login so you don’t have to sign in every time, or remember that you already viewed a particular property so we don’t show it again (aligning with our no-repeat listing promise). We may use both session cookies (which expire when you close the browser) and persistent cookies (that stay for a period unless deleted). We also use cookies to collect analytics data (see Analytics below) ￼. You can control cookies through your browser settings (e.g., to block or delete cookies), but note that some site features may not function properly without them.
	•	Analytics and Usage Data: We use third-party analytics tools (such as Google Analytics) to collect information about user interactions with our Services. This includes data on page views, time spent, buttons clicked, etc., which is often aggregated and anonymized. Google Analytics, for example, may capture demographic info or interests inferred from your browsing, but not your name or contact info ￼. We use this data to understand and improve user experience and features. Users can opt-out of Google Analytics for display advertising and customize Google Display Network ads via Google’s Ads Settings ￼.
	•	Advertising Partners: While currently 360Ghar does not show third-party ads, if in the future we partner with advertisers, they may use cookies or pixels to deliver relevant ads on our site or elsewhere. We will update this policy and obtain any necessary consents if advertising cookies are used.

C. Information from Third Parties
On occasion, we might receive information about you from other sources, which we add to our database:
	•	If you choose to integrate or sign up via a social media or Google account, we import the data you permit from that account (e.g., your profile name, profile picture, email).
	•	If a friend refers you to our platform (via a referral link or similar), we may receive your email or phone from them to send an invite (you will have the opportunity to opt out).
	•	We may work with third-party data providers or marketing partners that provide demographic information or interest data to help us enhance our user profiles and offer targeted suggestions. For example, we might get info about which users are first-time homebuyers vs. investors to tailor content.
	•	If you use a co-branded service or interact with a third-party service through our platform (like a loan assistant, legal documentation service, or interior design consultant introduced via 360Ghar), those third parties might share back information with us about the interaction (for instance, whether you availed their service, or generic outcome data), subject to their privacy terms and your agreements with them.

We treat any combined or received data under the same Privacy Policy terms as if you provided it, and we ensure any third-party sources are lawfully permitted to share such data with us.

Children’s Data: 360Ghar is not intended for minors. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 18 years of age. If you are under 18, please do not use or register on our app/website. We do not offer services to minors and require users to confirm they are 18 or older during sign-up ￼. If we learn that we have inadvertently collected personal information from a child under 18, we will delete it as soon as possible. Parents or guardians who believe their child may have provided us personal data should contact us (see Grievance/Contact section) to request deletion.

3. How We Use Your Information

We use the collected information for various lawful purposes. Under the DPDP Act, we primarily rely on your consent (for data you provide) and certain “legitimate uses” for processing ￼ ￼. The usage includes:
	•	To Provide and Improve Services: We process data to operate our platform and deliver features to you. For example, we use your location to show nearby properties, your search inputs to display matching listings, and your account data to personalize content. We remember properties you liked or passed to avoid repetition, fulfilling our core feature. We also use data for debugging, fixing errors, and improving usability and effectiveness of features (like enhancing the virtual tour quality or swipe interface based on usage patterns). This is considered necessary for the performance of our contract with you (to provide the Services you signed up for). It is also our legitimate interest to continuously better our offerings.
	•	Authentication and Security: We use personal identifiers (email, phone) to verify accounts (through OTPs or verification links) and to authenticate you at login. This helps prevent unauthorized access. We monitor login IPs and device info to detect suspicious logins and protect against fraud or abuse. Any unusual activity may trigger account verification steps for security.
	•	Facilitating Transactions and Communication: Your information will be used to facilitate communication between buyers and sellers (or landlords and tenants) with your consent. For instance, if you are interested in a property, we may share your name and contact with the Owner (or vice versa) to schedule a visit or negotiate, but typically via our agents or platform interface. We also use your contact info to send booking confirmations, visit reminders, and agreements drafts. If you schedule a visit, we may send you and the Owner an email/SMS with details. If a sale/rent agreement is being drawn, we might collect required data (like your PAN for the agreement) and share it with the legal documentation service preparing the contract. All such processing is done to fulfill your requests.
	•	Service Communication: We will use your email and phone number to send administrative or service messages: e.g., account welcome email, verification codes, password reset links, updates to Terms or Privacy Policy, alerts about changes to a property’s status, or support responses. These are transactional and not promotional in nature.
	•	Marketing and Promotions: We may use your contact details to send promotional content (where you have not opted out) such as newsletters, new property alerts, offers, or partner promotions that might interest you. For example, if you searched in a particular locality, we might notify you when a new listing in that area is added. We might also send general market trend reports or tips for home buyers. We ensure these messages are sent with appropriate consent as required by law ￼. You can unsubscribe from marketing emails via the provided link, and opt out of SMS/WhatsApp promotions by contacting support or using provided options. (Transactional communications, however, cannot be fully opted out of, as they relate to core service usage.)
	•	Content Moderation and Enforcement: Data such as your posted content and usage patterns may be reviewed to enforce our Terms and Content Guidelines. For example, if we receive a complaint that a user posted fraudulent information, we will use the data (and possibly share it with complainants or authorities) to investigate and take appropriate action (e.g., takedown, warning, or ban). We also may use automated systems to scan for violations (like detecting obscene language or malware in uploads). All these uses are in pursuit of legal compliance and maintaining a safe platform, consistent with the IT Rules requirements for intermediaries to moderate unlawful content ￼ ￼.
	•	Analytics and Personalization: To enhance user experience, we analyze how different users interact with our app. This may include generating aggregated insights like “most searched localities” or “typical time spent on virtual tours”. We might use your past behavior to personalize the content shown – e.g., ordering search results by relevance to you, or recommending similar properties to ones you liked. Such profiling is intended to benefit your experience. Under DPDP Act’s concept of legitimate use, since you voluntarily provided data and did not object to such use, we consider this a permitted use ￼. Nonetheless, you can contact us to object to certain personalization if you wish.
	•	Map and Location-Based Services: We use location data to provide map-based property discovery and driving directions. If you ask to see properties “near me” or use our map feature, we process your geolocation to fetch results around that coordinate. Location is also used to compute distance to points of interest (like distance from a property to nearest metro).
	•	Preventing Fraud/Illegal Activities: We may use personal data to prevent, detect, and investigate fraud, security breaches, or other potentially prohibited or illegal activities. For example, we may keep device fingerprints or use CAPTCHA to prevent bots, and use phone/email verification to deter scammers. If we suspect an account of money laundering or attempting to circumvent fees, we will analyze usage logs and may share information with law enforcement as required. This is a legal obligation and also under the DPDP Act could fall under necessary processing for law enforcement or public order ￼.
	•	Legal Compliance: Any information may be used to comply with applicable legal obligations, such as maintaining records for tax, responding to lawful requests by authorities, and fulfilling data retention duties. If a law or regulation requires us to collect certain information (for example, RERA regulations might require broker platforms to record agent registration details or transaction logs), we will collect and use data for that purpose. We also use contact details to provide notice and obtain consent as required by DPDP – e.g., presenting this privacy notice and offering choices ￼.

We ensure that data usage is minimized to what is necessary for each purpose and done in a fair and transparent manner ￼. If we intend to use your data for any new purpose not covered by this Policy, we will update this Policy or seek additional consent.

4. Sharing and Disclosure of Information

We are committed to maintaining the confidentiality of your personal information. We do not sell your personal data. However, we do share information in the following circumstances:
	•	With Counterparties to Transactions: When you express interest in a property or have an active deal, some information will be shared with the other party. For example, if you are a buyer interested in a seller’s property, with your consent, we may share your name and verified phone number or email with the seller (or their authorized representative) to enable site visits or negotiations. Similarly, a landlord and prospective tenant may receive each other’s contact details once they agree to proceed, to coordinate directly. We limit the data shared to what is necessary (usually name and contact, and any specific info needed like move-in date preferences). 360Ghar’s agents often facilitate these exchanges to protect privacy until a certain stage.
	•	With Service Providers and Partners: We employ trusted third-party companies and individuals to perform functions on our behalf. Examples include: hosting providers (for data storage and servers), SMS/email delivery services, analytics providers, customer support ticketing systems, marketing email platforms, identity verification services, payment gateway providers for online payments, and professional service partners (photographers, property inspection teams, movers, legal documentation firms, etc. that are involved in fulfilling the service). These third parties receive only the data necessary to perform their specific obligations. For instance, if we hire a photography contractor to shoot your property, we’ll share your address and contact to arrange the shoot. If an outside law firm or documentation service prepares an agreement, they will receive details like buyer and seller names, addresses, and property details needed for the contract. All such providers are contractually bound to protect your data and use it only for the agreed purpose.
	•	Within Corporate Group: Since 360Ghar is currently under YORD LLC (USA) and may operate through affiliated entities (including any future entity registered in India when the business expands), we may share data among our parent, subsidiaries, and affiliates as needed to operate the platform and provide services. All such group entities will honor the commitments of this Privacy Policy.
	•	For Legal Requirements: We may disclose personal information if required to do so by law or in the good faith belief that such action is necessary to (a) comply with a legal obligation, valid court order, or at the request of governmental authorities ￼; (b) enforce our Terms of Service or other agreements; (c) respond to claims that any content violates the rights of third parties; or (d) protect the rights, property, or personal safety of 360Ghar, our users, or the public. For example, under the IT Act and rules, if law enforcement sends us an order to provide user data related to a posted content that is unlawful, we will comply to the extent legally required ￼. Also, if a user defaults on fee payment and we pursue legal action, we might disclose that user’s information in court as needed.
	•	During Business Transfers: If 360Ghar or YORD LLC is involved in a merger, acquisition, asset sale, reorganization, bankruptcy, or other transaction, your data may be transferred as part of that deal. We will ensure the new owner continues to respect your personal data in line with this Privacy Policy (or notifies you of changes). You will be given notice and an opportunity to opt out if data will be handled in a fundamentally different way.
	•	With Consent: We will share your personal information with third parties when we have your specific consent to do so. For instance, if you opt-in to a special promotion with a housing loan company or interior design partner we collaborate with, we might share your contact and requirements with them so they can reach out to you. These scenarios will be clearly presented for your acceptance.

Sharing of Non-Personal Data: We may share aggregated or anonymized information (which cannot be used to identify you) with partners or the public. For example, we might publish market statistics like “average 2BHK rent in Mumbai is X” or share usage trends with a research firm. This data will not include any personally identifiable information.

International Transfers: The personal data we collect is primarily stored and processed within India. However, as our parent company is based in the US and we use some cloud services, your data may be transferred to and stored on servers located outside India, including the United States or other countries, for purposes such as cloud storage, backups, or processing by a specialized service provider. We assure that regardless of where data is processed, we take steps to ensure it is adequately protected to a standard commensurate with Indian law. If required under the DPDP Act or other law, we will only transfer personal data outside India in accordance with approved transfer mechanisms or after ensuring that the recipient country or entity provides comparable data protection ￼ ￼. By using our services, you consent to such transfer, storage, and processing of your information outside of India. We remain liable for the proper handling of your personal data by our overseas affiliates and service providers.

5. Data Retention

We retain your personal data only as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected, or as required or permitted by law. Concretely:
	•	Account Data: We will keep your profile and account information while your account is active. If you delete your account or it’s inactive for an extended period (we may define a threshold, e.g. 2 years of inactivity), we will initiate deletion of personal data, except where retention is required. We may retain a skeleton of basic data even after deletion for record-keeping (e.g., email and phone hashed for preventing re-registration by banned users, or maintaining logs of consents).
	•	Transaction Data: Information related to property transactions (like agreements, invoices, receipts of brokerage) may be retained for longer periods due to legal and accounting obligations – for instance, Indian tax laws or RERA may mandate retention of certain records for a number of years. We also retain transaction histories to address any disputes or legal claims that may arise later.
	•	Content: Posts or reviews you make may remain on the platform until you remove them or as part of platform data even after account deletion (in anonymized form, unless removal is requested). If content is removed for violations, we may retain copies internally to prevent future reposting or for legal evidence.
	•	Logs and Analytics: Server logs and analytics data are typically retained for a shorter period (often 6-24 months) unless needed for security analysis. We may keep security-related logs longer to investigate fraud or attacks.
	•	Backups: Your data might remain in secure backups for some additional time. If we restore from backups, we attempt to ensure deleted data is not restored, or if it is, we re-delete it promptly.

When personal data is no longer needed, we will either securely delete it or anonymize it. For example, if you withdraw consent and request deletion of certain info, we will erase it unless retention is legally required. According to DPDP Act requirements, we cease to retain personal data when the purpose is fulfilled and it is not required for legal reasons ￼. We also respect your right to request erasure (see Your Rights below).

6. Data Security

We take reasonable and appropriate security measures to protect your personal data from unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction ￼. These measures include:
	•	Encryption: Sensitive data (like passwords, which are hashed & salted) and certain fields may be stored in encrypted form. Our website is served over HTTPS, ensuring data in transit is encrypted.
	•	Access Controls: Personal data is accessible only to those employees, agents, and contractors who need access to perform their job duties (need-to-know basis). We implement role-based access control in our systems. The data viewable by property field agents through their app is limited to what they need (they might see your name and appointment time but not unrelated data).
	•	Secure Storage: We use reputable cloud service providers with robust security practices. Data centers have physical security and logical safeguards. Regular backups are maintained securely.
	•	Monitoring and Testing: We monitor our systems for possible vulnerabilities and attacks. We employ firewalls, intrusion detection systems, and perform periodic security audits and penetration tests to strengthen our platform’s security.
	•	Training and Policies: Our team members are trained on data protection best practices and are bound by confidentiality obligations. We maintain internal policies to handle data securely.
	•	Incident Response: In the unlikely event of a data breach or security incident, we have procedures in place to contain it and investigate. If a personal data breach is likely to result in harm to you, we will notify you and, if required, the appropriate authorities (like the Data Protection Board of India under the DPDP Act) as soon as feasible ￼.

Please note that while we strive to protect your data, no system can be 100% secure. You also play a role in security: keep your password confidential and use a unique, strong password. Do not share OTPs or verification codes with anyone. If you suspect any unauthorized access or have reason to believe your account is compromised, contact us immediately.

7. Your Rights and Choices

We respect your rights over your personal data. Under applicable law (including the DPDP Act), you have the following rights:
	•	Right to Access: You have the right to know what personal data we hold about you and to obtain a copy of that data ￼. Most of your basic account info is available for review in your profile section of our app/website. For additional data or a full export, you can make a request by contacting us (see Grievance section). We will provide the information in a reasonable time as required by law.
	•	Right to Correction: If any of your personal information is inaccurate or incomplete, you have the right to request correction or updating ￼. You can edit many details yourself in the app (like your profile info). For other data that you can’t change (e.g., if your phone number changed but account is tied to old number), contact us with proof of the correct information and we will update it. We strive to ensure our data is accurate and up-to-date ￼.
	•	Right to Erasure: You may request deletion of your personal data when it is no longer necessary for the purpose collected, or if you withdraw consent, or if you believe we are unlawfully processing it ￼. You can delete your account through the app settings or by contacting support. Upon such request, we will delete or anonymize your personal data that we are not legally required or permitted to retain. Note that removal from active systems might be prompt but deletion from backups might occur later; we will ensure the data is not restored or used from backups beyond retention period.
	•	Right to Withdraw Consent: For data processing that is based on your consent (e.g., receiving promotional communications, or using precise location), you have the right to withdraw consent at any time. You can opt out of marketing emails by unsubscribing, opt out of push notifications via app settings, or disable location sharing from your device settings. Withdrawal of consent will not affect processing already done, but we will stop the specific processing going forward. If withdrawal of certain consent means we can no longer provide you an aspect of Service (e.g., if you withdraw consent for us processing any of your personal data at all), we will inform you and it might result in account deletion if we cannot operate without that data.
	•	Right to Data Portability: To the extent applicable under Indian law, you may have the right to request a copy of your personal data in a machine-readable format, or to have it transmitted to another service provider, where technically feasible. This typically applies to data you provided to us directly. We will accommodate such requests as per legal requirements.
	•	Right to Grievance Redressal: If you have any grievances regarding your personal data processing, you have the right to lodge a complaint with our Grievance Officer (see Section 9) and expect a resolution within a timely manner ￼. If you are not satisfied with our response, under DPDP Act once fully in force, you may also have the right to file a complaint with the Data Protection Board of India.
	•	Right to Restrict or Object (if applicable): In certain situations, you may object to or ask us to limit processing of your data – for instance, if you contest the accuracy of data (until verified), or if you feel processing is not lawful. We will examine such requests and comply if required by law. For marketing, you can object at any time and we will stop sending marketing messages.

Verification: For any rights request, we will need to verify your identity to ensure that the person making the request is authorized. This might involve confirming via your registered email/phone or asking for additional verification info.

No Discrimination: We will not discriminate against you for exercising any of these rights. However, do understand that some services require certain data – for example, if you request deletion of all your data, we cannot continue to provide services, and deletion of essential data will be treated as a request to delete your account.

Retention of Requests: We may keep a record of rights requests and our responses for legal compliance and record-keeping.

8. Content Removal and Takedown Requests

If you believe any content on the 360Ghar platform (including property listings, user reviews, comments, etc.) violates your rights or is unlawful, you can request its takedown. Our Takedown Policy (see next section) describes the procedure for making such complaints under the IT Act’s intermediary rules and other applicable laws. For instance, if you are a copyright holder and find your photo used without permission, or if someone posted defamatory information about you, you may notify our Grievance Officer. We will acknowledge such complaints within 24 hours and dispose of them within 15 days or earlier as required ￼. Unlawful content (such as obscene material, or content violating sovereignty of India, etc.) once notified through proper channels (court order or government notice) will be removed or disabled within 36 hours ￼. We may also remove content on a voluntary basis if it violates our policies, without waiting for formal orders ￼.

For more details on how to submit takedown requests and how we handle them, please refer to the “Content Takedown Policy” section of our documentation below.

9. Grievance Redressal and Contact Information

In compliance with India’s IT (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules and DPDP Act, we have appointed a Grievance Officer as the point of contact for user grievances, including those related to personal data.

Grievance Officer:
Name: [To be Appointed]
Email: grievance@360ghar.com (or you may use support@360ghar.com for general support and it will be escalated accordingly)
Contact Address: (we will update with the office address once 360Ghar establishes an office in India; currently, under YORD LLC, USA. In the interim, email is the preferred contact.)
Working Hours: 10 AM to 6 PM, Monday–Friday (excluding public holidays).

If you have any concerns, complaints, or inquiries regarding:
	•	misuse of the platform,
	•	content posted by other users,
	•	issues with our handling of your personal data,
	•	or any breach of these Terms or Privacy Policy,

please contact the Grievance Officer at the provided email. You should provide your name, contact information, and a detailed description of your grievance, along with any supporting evidence (screenshots, URLs, etc.). Our Grievance Officer will acknowledge receipt of your complaint within 24 hours ￼. We aim to resolve or address your complaint within 15 days of receipt ￼, or as per applicable law. In case of data-related grievances under DPDP, we will also adhere to any timelines prescribed by that Act or its rules.

For general questions about our policies or your data, you can also contact us at support@360ghar.com. We will do our best to assist you.

If you do not receive a satisfactory resolution to your grievance from us, you may explore remedies such as approaching the statutory authorities. For personal data issues, once the Data Protection Board is operational under DPDP Act, you may escalate complaints there. For content-related issues, the IT Rules provide for an appeal to the Grievance Appellate Committee (GAC) within 30 days if you are not satisfied with the outcome of your complaint handling ￼. We will provide requisite information on how to file such an appeal in our response if applicable.

title: Content Guidelines
content:
Content Guidelines

(These Guidelines are provided to ensure a safe, respectful, and law-abiding community on 360Ghar. They apply to all user-generated content and activity on the platform, including property descriptions by owners, user comments, feedback, and any media uploaded.)

User Responsibilities: When posting content on 360Ghar, you are responsible for the material you share. Always ensure you have the right to post the content (it should be your own creation or you have obtained necessary permissions). Content should be relevant to real estate and helpful to other users. We encourage honest opinions and information, but it must be shared legally and respectfully.

Prohibited Content: The following types of content are strictly prohibited on 360Ghar. This list is based on Indian law standards for online content ￼ ￼ and our own community standards:
	•	Intellectual Property Violations: Do not upload content that you do not own or have permission to use. This includes photos, videos, text, or documents belonging to someone else. For example, copying a competitor’s property listing or using photographs from another website without permission is forbidden ￼. If you post content, you affirm it is yours or you have the rights. Unauthorized use of copyrighted material, trademarks, or other proprietary content will be removed upon notice and could lead to account penalties.
	•	Privacy Violations and Personal Data: Do not share personally identifying information about another person without their consent. This includes phone numbers, email addresses, physical addresses, Aadhaar/PAN numbers, etc. in public content. Also refrain from posting photos of individuals (owners, tenants, etc.) without consent, or any content that invades someone’s privacy or security.
	•	Harassment and Hate Speech: Content that bullies, harasses, or intimidates an individual or group is prohibited. You must not use slurs or insults based on someone’s race, caste, religion, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic ￼. This includes threats of harm or doxxing someone’s personal information. 360Ghar has zero tolerance for hate speech or any content inciting violence or prejudice.
	•	Obscene or Pornographic Material: Do not post any obscene content, pornography, or sexually explicit material ￼. Property listings should remain professional. Avoid images or descriptions that are vulgar or meant to sexually gratify. Any depiction of child sexual abuse or content sexualizing minors will be immediately removed and reported to authorities.
	•	Defamation and False Information: Users should not post false statements that could harm the reputation of a person or business (defamation). While reviews and feedback (e.g., “The property was not as described”) are allowed, stick to factual, first-hand experiences. Avoid unverified rumors or accusations. Importantly, do not knowingly post “patently false or misleading information” that could be perceived as fact ￼. For example, falsely claiming “This broker is a fraud who steals money” without evidence could be defamatory and is not allowed.
	•	Fraudulent or Illegal Activity: Any content that promotes or depicts illegal activities is banned. This includes advising how to circumvent laws, encouraging violence, money laundering, gambling or betting operations, etc ￼. Listings for properties that cannot legally be sold/rented, or schemes that look like scams (like “invest here for quick returns” unrelated to actual property use) will be removed. Users should not use our platform to advertise any service that is illegal or requires a license they do not possess.
	•	Spam and Advertising: The comments and forums on 360Ghar are not to be used for advertising third-party products or services. Do not repeatedly post the same comment or send unwanted messages to other users. Creating listings or content primarily to drive traffic to external sites (affiliate marketing, etc.) is not allowed. Keep content relevant to the property or real estate topic at hand. We also consider posting links to malicious sites or attempting phishing as serious violations.
	•	Violence and Extremism: Graphic content that depicts violence, gore, or encourages harm to anyone is disallowed. Similarly, any content supporting terrorist organizations or extremist ideologies, calling for insurgency or communal violence, etc., will be removed and could be reported to law enforcement. Content that threatens the unity, integrity, security or sovereignty of India or friendly relations with foreign States is illegal to publish ￼ and strictly forbidden on our platform.
	•	Child Safety: Any content suggestive of child abuse, exploitation, or that depicts minors in inappropriate contexts will be removed. Users should not post any personal details of minors. We comply with laws on removal of such content and will report incidents to authorities as required.
	•	Impersonation and Authenticity: Do not impersonate other people or entities, or falsely claim to represent an organization. For instance, do not create an account pretending to be another real estate company or a government official. Also, ensure property listings are authentic – do not list a property that does not exist or that you have no authority to list. Knowingly deceiving users (like a fake listing to gather contact info) is a bannable offense.
	•	Software and Security Violations: Users are not allowed to upload any software or files that could harm other users’ devices or the platform’s integrity, such as viruses, worms, Trojan horses, corrupted files, or any other similar software ￼. Additionally, you should not exploit the platform to engage in any sort of hacking, data mining beyond allowed use, or security testing without permission.
	•	Prohibited Transactions: Any attempt to use our platform to facilitate transactions that are illegal under Indian law is forbidden. For example, do not advertise properties for sale which are government-owned and not for sale, or solicit bribes (“under the table” money) through our messaging. If any content hints at corrupt practices or violates any specific sector regulations (like black money in transactions), it will be removed and possibly flagged to authorities.
	•	Miscellaneous Unacceptable Content: This includes content that deceives or misleads the addressee about the origin of the message (like spoofed messages), or content that is “junk” or mass messaging. Also, anything that violates any law for the time being in force is disallowed ￼. Users should also refrain from posting extremely off-topic content in places not meant for it (e.g., discussing politics in a property review – aside from how it relates to the property location – may be moderated if disruptive).

Language and Tone: Keep the language civil. Even when posting negative feedback, refrain from profanity or personal attacks. We encourage constructive criticism and problem-solving attitude. Content that includes profanity or slurs may be edited or removed by moderators, especially if directed at an individual. If you’re frustrated about a property or an experience, express it without violating these guidelines.

User-Generated Images/Media: If you upload photos (for example, perhaps in a review or Q&A section in the future) or property images, ensure they are relevant and do not violate any rules above. Do not overlay text on images that could be misleading or promotional. No watermarks of other companies on photos. For 360° tours or videos, similar rules apply: nothing obscene, no unauthorized people in frame without consent, etc.

Moderation Actions: Content that violates these guidelines may be subject to action including removal or editing. 360Ghar’s moderation team (including automated filters and human reviewers) can remove content without prior notice if it’s against our policies or the law ￼. In cases of minor infractions, we might notify the user and advise adjustments. Serious or repeat violations can lead to account suspension or permanent ban. For illegal content, we might also inform law enforcement or comply with judicial or governmental orders for removal ￼.

Reporting Mechanism: We rely on our community to maintain these standards. If you spot content that you believe violates the Content Guidelines or is illegal/harmful, please report it using the “Report” feature available next to the content, or email our Grievance Officer (with details like content link and concern). Our team will review the report promptly. As per our Grievance mechanism, we acknowledge complaints within 24 hours and resolve in 15 days or sooner for content issues ￼. For certain categories (like nudity, impersonation, etc.), we may act even faster if possible. If needed, we may reach out to the reporting party for more info.

Fair Usage: Don’t abuse the reporting feature. False reports or frivolous grievances (like trying to take down a competitor’s listing that doesn’t violate rules) are discouraged. In fact, the DPDP Act assigns duties to Data Principals (users) such as not registering false or frivolous grievances ￼ – similar ethos here, misuse of the grievance process can itself be considered a violation if done maliciously.

By maintaining these standards together, we can ensure that 360Ghar remains a trustworthy and pleasant platform for all users. Thank you for contributing positively to our community.

title: Content Takedown Policy
content:
Content Takedown Policy

360Ghar is committed to respecting legal requirements and user rights concerning content posted on our platform. This Takedown Policy outlines how unlawful or policy-violating content can be reported and how 360Ghar will respond. It covers intellectual property infringement claims (like copyright), defamation, privacy violations, and other illegal content as defined under Indian law and our Content Guidelines.

1. Reporting Unlawful Content: If you believe any content on 360Ghar is unlawful or violates your rights, you should notify our Grievance Officer with the following details:
	•	Your Information: Name, contact details (email and phone number), and a statement of your relationship to the content (e.g., “I am the person defamed in the comment” or “I am the copyright owner of the photo used in this listing”). If you are acting on behalf of someone (like an attorney or authorized agent), provide proof of authority.
	•	Content Identification: Clearly identify the content in question. Provide URLs/specific location in app, screenshots if possible, and describe it. For example, “Property listing ID 12345 – the third photo is my copyrighted image” or “Review comment by user X on [date] contains defamatory statements about me.”
	•	Nature of Complaint: Explain why the content is unlawful or violates rights. If it’s copyright or trademark infringement, include proof of ownership (like registration certificates or links to original work) and a statement that the use is unauthorized. If it’s defamation, explain why it’s false and injurious. If privacy, specify what personal information is exposed without consent. If it falls under categories like obscenity, hate speech, etc., explain briefly (we likely can tell by inspection, but context helps).
	•	Legal Basis (if any): You can reference relevant laws or court orders if you have any. E.g., “This content violates Section 67 of IT Act (obscenity)” or “Delhi High Court injuncted this content via order XYZ, copy attached.” If you have a court order directing removal of certain content, attaching it will expedite removal as per Section 79 of IT Act safe harbour rules ￼.
	•	Declaration: For IP infringement or defamation claims, it’s helpful (and may be legally required for DMCA-like process) to include a statement that: “I have a good faith belief that use of the material complained of is not authorized by the owner, its agent, or the law” (for IP) or “The information in this notice is true to my knowledge and I am complaining lawfully and not for any improper purpose.” Also include a statement that you are prepared to indemnify 360Ghar against any false claims in your notice (this deters fraudulent complaints).

Send this notice to grievance@360ghar.com. You may also send physical notices to our office when established (currently email is more effective). We encourage email for speed.

2. 360Ghar’s Response and Timeline: Once we receive a content complaint:
	•	Our Grievance Officer will acknowledge receipt within 24 hours (per IT Rules) ￼, usually via email reply. We might ask for clarifications if the notice is incomplete.
	•	We will review the complaint for validity. If it clearly falls under prohibited categories (like nudity, impersonation, etc.), we may remove or disable access to the content immediately as a precaution. For example, if someone reports a user has posted their PAN card image publicly, we’d likely remove it straight away as a privacy violation.
	•	For more complex issues (like defamation or copyright where context is needed), we may evaluate the content and possibly seek legal advice. We might also inform the user who posted the content and seek their response, especially in defamation cases – this is not always required, but for fairness we may do so. However, if we believe the content is prima facie infringing or unlawful, we reserve the right to take it down first to limit liability and then hear any counter-notice from the uploader later ￼.
	•	In line with Indian law, if we receive an order from a court or an appropriate government agency directing removal of certain content (for reasons such as sovereignty, security, etc.), we will comply within 36 hours of receiving that order ￼. For example, a court injunction on defamatory content will be executed promptly. Similarly, any government notification under law (by an authorized agency) about unlawful content will be honored in the stipulated time.
	•	Absent a court/government order, user complaints of policy violations or illegal content will be resolved within 15 days from date of receipt ￼ (as mandated by IT Rules for grievance redressal). We strive to act faster whenever possible, especially for clear-cut issues. You will be informed of the resolution: e.g., content removed, or if we decide to retain it (with reasoning, unless obvious). If content is removed, we’ll usually also notify the user who posted it and provide them reason and possibly your complaint details (minus personal contact info, unless necessary).
	•	If the original poster believes their content was removed unfairly (say, they have rights or the complaint was incorrect), they can file a counter-notice or appeal with us. Our Grievance Officer will review such appeals. In some cases, disputes about content might need resolution in court – we may take a neutral stance and let the parties resolve legal claims externally if it’s not obviously illegal to us. But as an intermediary, our bias will be toward erring on side of caution (removing contested content to respect rights and laws, given safe harbour conditions).

3. Intellectual Property Takedowns:
	•	Copyright: 360Ghar respects copyright owners. If you believe your copyrighted work is being used on our platform without permission, send us a copyright infringement notice (containing the details outlined above). We may disable access to the content while evaluating. We follow a notice-and-takedown approach akin to DMCA principles (though under Indian law). The user who posted the content may send a counter-notice if they believe they had the right (for instance, fair use or a license). We might restore the content if a valid counter-notice is received, unless the complainant seeks a court order within a reasonable time.
