Repeat copyright infringers will have their accounts terminated.
	•	Trademark: If you own a trademark and find its unauthorized use in a way that confuses users (e.g., someone falsely claiming to be your brand or using your logo), let us know. We may remove or moderate content that misuses trademarks in a misleading manner (for example, a fake profile claiming to be an official account of a known builder, or someone using a company logo in their listing to falsely suggest endorsement).
	•	Other IP: For patent infringement or trade secret issues (less likely on a real estate platform), contact us with necessary info and we will address on a case-by-case basis.

4. Other Unlawful Content: If content falls under categories like those listed in our Content Guidelines (hate speech, obscene material, threats to national security, etc.), users can report as described. We will evaluate under the prism of applicable law (IPC, IT Act, etc.). For instance, any content that appears to offend religious sentiments or could incite violence will be assessed under relevant sections (like 295A of IPC, etc.) and if found violative, removed and possibly reported.

5. Transparency: We maintain records of all content complaints and takedown actions. As an intermediary, we also publish compliance reports periodically (if mandated by law) mentioning the number of grievances received and actions taken ￼. Complainants’ identities are kept confidential in these reports. For significant social media intermediaries, a grievance appellate committee route is available (though 360Ghar is not a social media platform per se, if ever categorized as such due to user content volume, we will comply accordingly).

6. Good Samaritan and Voluntary Removal: 360Ghar reserves the right to remove any content that we independently discover to violate our policies or the law, even if no complaint is received ￼. Such removal is done in good faith to keep the platform safe. We also may cooperate with law enforcement proactively if we find content that indicates a serious unlawful act (like human trafficking, explicit pornographic posts, etc.). However, we are not under an obligation to monitor all content at all times – as an intermediary, we primarily act when notified, except for certain automated filtering (e.g., known pornographic hash lists, etc., if implemented).

7. Restoring Content: If content was removed due to a notice that later turns out to be false or withdrawn, we will consider restoring it (and re-enabling user’s account if it was suspended). We will notify users accordingly. In case of counter-notices for copyright: if a user submits a sworn counter-notice that the removal was mistaken, we might restore the content in 10 business days unless the original complainant informs us they have filed court action to restrain the user. This follows a standard safe harbor process.

8. No Liability for Removal: As stated in our Terms, 360Ghar cannot be held liable for removing or disabling access to content in response to any notice or on voluntary basis ￼. By our policies, we may remove content that even potentially violates laws to avoid legal risk. Users have agreed that such takedowns by 360Ghar do not breach any obligation to them. We base our decisions on the information at hand and our legal responsibilities, aiming to err on the side of safety and legality.

This Takedown Policy is meant to comply with Section 3(2) of the Intermediary Guidelines Rules 2021 and other relevant laws. It may be updated as laws change (for example, if the new DPDP Act requires new procedures for personal data related content takedown, we will incorporate that).

If you have any questions about this policy or need to follow up on a report you made, please contact grievance@360ghar.com with reference to your original complaint. We appreciate your cooperation in keeping the 360Ghar platform trustworthy and lawful.

title: Grievance Redressal Mechanism
content:
Grievance Redressal Mechanism

In accordance with the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Rules, 2021 and forthcoming provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, 360Ghar has established the following Grievance Redressal Mechanism. We are dedicated to addressing user grievances efficiently and transparently.

Grievance Officer:
Name: [Name of Grievance Officer]
Designation: Grievance Officer – 360Ghar
Contact Email: grievance@360ghar.com
Contact Address: (To be updated – currently servicing via email. Office address will be added once Indian operations are registered.)
Contact Number: (If applicable, will be provided in the future. In the meantime, email is the primary channel.)

Roles of Grievance Officer: The Grievance Officer is responsible for:
	•	Receiving and acknowledging user complaints and grievances relating to the 360Ghar platform, including those about content, accounts, or data privacy ￼.
	•	Reviewing and resolving these grievances, which may involve content removal, user communication, or other remedial actions as appropriate.
	•	Serving as the point of contact for governmental or legal notices addressed to the platform.
	•	Guiding the platform’s compliance with grievance timelines and reporting obligations.

How to Submit a Grievance: Users (or victims of content) can submit grievances by:
	•	Email: Send a detailed description of your issue to grievance@360ghar.com. Please include your name, contact info, and relevant details (such as usernames, property listing IDs, date of incident, screenshots). This helps us understand and act on the complaint.
	•	In-App Support: (If available) You may use the support/chat section of our app to register a complaint. Our support team will escalate formal grievances to the Grievance Officer.
	•	Mail: Once we have a physical office in India, you may send written complaints to the Contact Address of the Grievance Officer. (Currently, use electronic means for fastest response.)

Grievance Categories: You may raise complaints including, but not limited to:
	•	Objectionable or illegal content on the platform (refer to our Content Guidelines for what is not allowed).
	•	Harassment or misuse by another user (e.g., someone spamming or attempting to make a deal outside the platform).
	•	Technical issues that severely impact your use of the service or cause some loss.
	•	Data privacy concerns (e.g., you suspect misuse of your personal data or have a complaint about a data breach).
	•	Any other matter concerning the platform’s offerings or your user experience that has not been resolved via normal customer support.

Acknowledgment and Timeline: Our Grievance Officer will acknowledge your complaint within 24 hours of receipt, either with an automated response or a personal email ￼. We will include a ticket/reference number for your complaint for tracking. We aim to resolve the issue within 15 days from the date of receipt of the grievance ￼, as mandated by the IT Rules. Resolution means we will investigate, take appropriate action (e.g., removing content, blocking a user, fixing a bug), and respond to you with the outcome or steps taken.

For example:
	•	If you complain that a certain listing contains false information and is repetitive, we might investigate, verify with our team, and if found true, remove or correct the listing and inform you of the action within the timeframe.
	•	If you raise a privacy concern, like wanting to know what data of yours we have or requesting deletion, we will respond with the information or confirmation of deletion as appropriate, within the time frame (likely sooner for straightforward requests).

Expedited Handling: Certain types of grievances will be addressed on a priority basis:
	•	Grievances involving personal safety or legality, such as someone posting your personal address or an image without consent, or content that can cause imminent harm, will be acted upon as quickly as possible, potentially well within the 15-day outer limit. Some issues might be resolved in 1-3 days if urgent.
	•	If the complaint pertains to content that falls under severe categories (e.g., sexual content, terrorism, etc.), we will remove/disable access within 24 hours as required by law, especially content concerning sexual exposure of individuals (per IT Rules) or CSAM. We will also initiate requisite reporting to authorities for such cases.
	•	Data breach related complaints: If you report a suspected data breach, our security team will treat it as high priority, investigate immediately, and we will update you on the findings or resolution (and also notify the Data Protection Board if required by DPDP Act) ￼.

User Support Ticket Integration: For efficiency, any complaint emailed to the grievance address may also be logged in our internal support ticketing system. You might receive updates through that system. Please check your email (including spam folder) for responses.

Grievance Appellate: According to the recent amendments, if you as a complainant are not satisfied with the resolution or it’s not resolved within the specified time, you can appeal the decision. The Government of India has established Grievance Appellate Committees (GAC) to handle such appeals for digital platforms.
	•	Appeal Process: You may file an appeal to the GAC within 30 days from either the date of our decision or the expiry of the 15-day resolution period, whichever is earlier ￼. The details for how to submit to the GAC will be provided on the government’s designated digital portal (once operational). We will also provide a link or instructions on our website when available.
	•	The GAC is expected to resolve appeals expeditiously, within 30 days of filing. The GAC’s decision, if any, will be binding and we will comply with any order from the GAC in terms of reinstating content, apology to user, or any other remedy.
	•	We truly hope to resolve issues without the need for escalation. Our goal is your satisfaction and compliance with law.

Record Keeping: We maintain a log of all grievances received and their resolution status. This includes the date received, content of the complaint, actions taken, and date of closure. We also generate periodic compliance reports (monthly, if required) which will include: number of grievances received, number disposed of, and summaries of results ￼. These reports (minus personal data) may be published on our website as required by law.

DPDP Act Compliance: Under the DPDP Act, users (Data Principals) have a right to grievance redressal for personal data issues ￼. Our mechanism above covers that. If we fail to address a data-related grievance satisfactorily, you can escalate to the Data Protection Board of India once established ￼. We will cooperate with any inquiries or proceedings initiated by the Board.

No Retaliation: 360Ghar assures users that raising a grievance will not lead to discrimination or denial of service. We treat complaints confidentially and with importance. Even if the complaint is against us (like “my data is misused by 360Ghar”), it will be handled impartially.

Contact for Law Enforcement: Though not directly user grievance, it’s relevant to mention: Our Grievance Officer is also the nodal person for interaction with law enforcement or authorized government agencies for any orders or requisitions (for example, seeking user data or content takedowns under lawful process). We will verify any such request is from an authorized source before complying.

Feedback on Grievance Process: We continuously improve. If you have suggestions on how we can enhance our grievance mechanism, you can let us know as well. This can be done via the support channels.

In summary, we are here to listen and resolve. Your concerns matter, and we aim to build a platform where issues are few, but when they arise, they are properly dealt with. These documented policies ensure that as we grow, we remain accountable to you and the law.

Thank you for reading through our comprehensive policies. 360Ghar is devoted to revolutionizing the real estate experience in India while ensuring legal compliance and user trust. By using our platform, you agree to these terms and policies which are designed to protect all parties and foster a reliable community.
